pr.title: More matching of lbcommand and some renaming
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed the lb command source/header/asm unit from plural lbcommands to singular lbcommand, renamed SubactionEvent symbols and SubactionInfo to Command_* and CommandInfo, and moved one more command handler out of standalone assembly into src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c as Command_08. The PR updated the command function pointer table in asm/data.s and obj_files.mk to keep the build using the renamed C and asm objects. Evidence is limited to the diff; there were no PR body notes or review comments.
what_changed: Renamed SubactionEvent_00 to Command_00 and SubactionEvent_04 to Command_04.
Added Command_08 to the C source and header. Its non-matching C form computes timer as the masked command data value minus frame_count, then advances data_position.
Removed the corresponding func_80005994 assembly body from the lbcommand assembly file, replacing it with C/inline-asm coverage as Command_08.
Updated asm/data.s command table lbl_803B9840 entries from SubactionEvent_00, SubactionEvent_04, and func_80005994 to Command_00, Command_04, and Command_08.
Updated obj_files.mk TEXT_O_FILES from lbcommands.o paths to lbcommand.o paths.
smart_moves: Kept the function pointer table in asm/data.s synchronized with the new symbol names, preventing stale SubactionEvent_* and func_* references.
Used a NON_MATCHING C version alongside inline assembly for Command_04 and Command_08, preserving readable intent while keeping the matching build path available.
Converted a raw func_80005994 assembly routine into a semantically named Command_08 implementation and removed its separate assembly listing.
Renamed the file, struct, and function family together, which improves grepability and avoids mixed lbcommands/lbcommand terminology.
Preserved the observed data layout offsets in CommandInfo comments while changing names, reducing risk during header refactoring.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a decomp unit, update source file names, assembly file names, headers, object lists, and data tables in the same PR to avoid broken symbol or build references.
Command dispatch tables are good evidence for naming: the table lbl_803B9840 maps opcode-like entries to Command_00, Command_04, and Command_08.

For small command handlers involving float conversion from packed integer command data, inline assembly can be used to preserve exact codegen while the NON_MATCHING branch documents the intended C behavior.
Removing a func_8000xxxx label from assembly should be paired with a semantically named C symbol in the dispatch table so future work tracks the decompiled coverage.
Header renames should include typedef and prototype renames together so downstream call sites see one consistent API.
naming_conventions: Use singular lbcommand for this source/header/asm unit rather than plural lbcommands.
Use CommandInfo for the command interpreter state struct instead of SubactionInfo.
Use Command_XX naming for command handlers keyed by command/opcode value, as shown by Command_00, Command_04, and Command_08.
Avoid leaving generic func_80005994 names once a command table role is known.

Field names in CommandInfo include timer, frame_count, data_position, loop_count, loop_count_dup, and unk_x18; the former EventReturn field was renamed to unk_x10 in this diff, suggesting its purpose was not yet confidently known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Command_04 and Command_08 use inline PowerPC assembly under the matching path and clearer C under #ifdef NON_MATCHING.

The handlers load command data through info->data_position, mask with clrlwi r4, r4, 6 corresponding to & 0x3ffffff, convert the integer through the 0x4330/lfd double-conversion idiom using lbl_804D79E0, then store a float timer.
Command_04 adds the decoded command value to the current timer; Command_08 subtracts frame_count from the decoded command value.
Both command handlers advance data_position by 4 bytes/one u32 after consuming command data.
The PR deleted the standalone assembly for func_80005994 after adding Command_08 in C/inline assembly, reducing unmatched assembly in asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s.
review_feedback: No review comments, issue comments, or PR body text were present in the provided context.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for CommandInfo unk_x10 to see whether the field was renamed once its purpose became known.
Search for additional Command_XX handlers in lbcommand to track expansion of the command interpreter decompilation.
Search for lbl_803B9840 updates to identify the full command dispatch table and opcode naming scheme.
Compare later matching attempts for Command_04 and Command_08 to see whether the inline assembly was replaced by matching C.
